Ad Text:
Location: Carseldine, 4034
Contract Duration: Through to May 2027
Flexibility: Hybrid working model

Our Client:
Our client is a large and complex organisation delivering a significant enterprise transformation program. They focus on improving systems, processes, and user experiences to better support their stakeholders and community outcomes.

The Role:
As a Change Analyst, you will support the successful adoption of a major program by assessing change impacts, driving business readiness, and contributing to key change and adoption activities. You will work closely with stakeholders, project teams, and functional specialists to ensure people are prepared to transition to new systems, processes, and ways of working.

Responsibilities:

Conduct change impact assessments, identify risks, and support business readiness for systems, processes, and role changes
Develop and maintain change management artefacts, including plans, readiness checklists, learning materials, and communications
Support transition planning, cutover activities, hypercare, and early-life support to ensure smooth adoption
Build strong relationships with stakeholders, facilitating workshops, briefings, and engagement sessions
Assist in learning needs analysis, develop training materials, coordinate delivery, and evaluate learning effectiveness
Monitor and report on change progress, risks, and issues, contributing to continuous improvement

Skills & Experience:
Mandatory

5+ years' experience in change management or a similar role
Proven experience supporting organisational change within projects or programs
Strong experience in change impact assessments and business readiness activities
Experience working in government or complex environments
Excellent stakeholder engagement, facilitation, and communication skills
Advanced Microsoft Office skills, particularly PowerPoint

Desirable

Tertiary qualifications in business, communications, or related discipline
Change management certification (e.g. Prosci)
Experience in large-scale digital, ICT, or enterprise implementations
Familiarity with LMS platforms and e-learning tools
Experience with tools such as SharePoint Online, Adobe Creative Suite, Visio, Viva Engage, and Forms

Benefits:

Work on a high-impact, large-scale transformation program
Collaborate with experienced professionals in a supportive team
Flexible working arrangements - 50% hybrid

About the Company
Davidson is partnering with a leading organisation in the superannuation sector who is a Digital Business Analyst for a 12-month fixed term contract. This role sits within a major transformation program and will support digital delivery across customer-facing initiatives, with a particular focus on front-end change.

This is a strong opportunity for a BA who enjoys working closely with product, engineering and SME teams in an agile environment, and who can bring structure, clarity and momentum to digital delivery streams.

About the Role
The Digital Business Analyst will play a key role in supporting digital workstreams across a broader transformation program. Operating within agile squads, this role will focus on translating business needs into clear, actionable artefacts that enable delivery teams to move efficiently and confidently.

The successful candidate will be responsible for writing epics and user stories, refining requirements, and working closely with product, engineering and subject matter experts to support front-end digital initiatives. This role will suit someone who is comfortable bridging business and technical teams and who understands how strong analysis supports better digital outcomes.

Key responsibilities will include:

Writing and maintaining epics, user stories and acceptance criteria
Supporting digital delivery streams across a large transformation program
Partnering with product, engineering and SME teams to refine and clarify requirements
Working closely with agile squads to support prioritisation and delivery readiness
Translating business needs into clear, user-focused requirements for front-end initiatives
Supporting backlog refinement, sprint planning and ongoing delivery activities
Contributing to the improvement of digital customer experiences through well-defined requirements
Liaising across business and technical stakeholders to ensure alignment across delivery

About You
To be successful in this role, the ideal candidate will be an experienced Digital Business Analyst with strong agile delivery capability and experience working on front-end digital initiatives. Exposure to superannuation, broader business domain knowledge, and an understanding of UX principles will be highly regarded.

The ideal background will include:

Proven experience as a Digital Business Analyst in agile delivery environments
Strong experience writing epics, user stories and acceptance criteria
Experience supporting digital or front-end delivery streams
Ability to work effectively with product, engineering and SME teams
Strong stakeholder engagement and requirements refinement capability
Exposure to superannuation or financial services environments will be preferred
Understanding of UX and customer-centred digital delivery will be advantageous
Technical experience, including exposure to integration-focused environments, will be highly regarded


The Benefits
This is an excellent opportunity to join a significant transformation program and contribute to digital initiatives that will shape the customer and business experience.

Benefits include:

12-month fixed term contract within a major multi year transformation program
Opportunity to work on high-impact digital and front-end initiatives
Hybrid working arrangements - CBD office - 8 days per month

Job Description


Established in 1988, Rest is one of Australia’s largest profit-to-member superannuation funds.

We support more than two million members, with around $100 billion of funds under management and are recognised as a responsible investment leader*. We believe when members understand and engage with their super, they’re more likely to get a better retirement outcome.

Everything we do at Rest is underpinned by our values and behaviours, we want to Be Daring, Keep it Simple, Take Action and Have Grit. To put it simply we want our people to thrive and love the work they do.

The Head of Voice of the Customer (VOC) is responsible for leading and embedding the organisation’s understanding of member needs, expectations and experiences across the end‑to‑end superannuation journey.

The role leads and evolves the VOC strategy, member / employer research and insight ecosystem that captures, interprets and embeds member sentiment - leveraging research, data, analytics and feedback channels to drive actionable insights.

By championing a member‑led culture and partnering across product, marketing, service, workplace, digital and operations teams, the role ensures decisions are informed by clear, evidence‑based member insights, driving improved member experience, trust, engagement and long‑term member outcomes.

Key Accountabilities/Responsibilities

Lead Enterprise Voice of the Customer Strategy

Develop and execute Rest’s enterprise-wide VOC strategy, ensuring the systematic and consistent capture of member and employer feedback across all channels.
Establish and maintain a unified VOC framework that supports strategic planning, service design, and continuous improvement initiatives.
Manage all VOC data pipelines, feedback systems, and measurement programs including NPS, CSAT, complaints analysis, survey programs, journey analytics and unstructured data capture.
Expand listening programs across digital, contact centre, employer interactions, adviser networks, and emerging channels, in line with strategic priorities.
Influence business owners to respond to VOC findings with meaningful action, tracking improvements against defined metrics.

Lead Member Research, Insights and Analytics

Lead qualitative and quantitative research programs that uncover actionable insights about member needs, behaviours and pain points.
Develop robust insight methodologies (e.g. segment analysis, journey diagnostics, sentiment analytics, behavioural insights) to support evidence‑based decision making across the organisation.
Ensure insights are translated into practical recommendations that drive material improvement in member outcomes.
Drive enterprise awareness of member needs through storytelling, dashboards, insights packs and strategic presentations.

Understand the Member Experience

Across key member journeys, deeply understand the end-to-end member experience by channel and interaction type. Identify and monitor key metrics and feedback loops that allow the early identification of friction or poor experience.
Highlight systemic issues and opportunities at both journey and strategic levels.
Work closely with Strategy, DTD, Risk, Services, and Product teams to ensure member voice is embedded into decision-making, prioritization and delivery.

Lead Experience Design

Map signature experiences aligned to strategic priorities to understand current state and future opportunities.
Lead the experience design function working with business teams to evolve member experiences to meet the needs of member and employer audiences into the future.

Govern Insight Quality, Integrity and Compliance

Ensure data accuracy, methodological rigour and compliance with privacy, regulatory and ethical standards across all VOC and research activities.
Maintain measurement consistency across channels and journeys, ensuring comparability and high data integrity.

Build a High Performing Team

Lead, coach, and develop a multidisciplinary team spanning research, insights, analytics, and CX measurement.
Foster a culture of curiosity, evidence‑based thinking, accountability, and continuous learning.

Qualifications


Relevant Marketing, Business, Commerce or Finance degree or equivalent

Required experience, understanding or credentials including:

Extensive Financial Services Expertise: Experience within superannuation or financial services.
Strategic and Commercial Acumen: Proven ability to apply commercial judgment to shape strategic direction, make critical decisions, and deliver outcomes to defined timeframes.
Customer-Centric Leadership: Demonstrated success in leading teams focused on member and customer experience, including experience designing and embedding VOC frameworks. Human centred design leadership experience preferred.
Collaborative Leadership: Strong influencing skills with the ability to lead through others, fostering alignment and collaboration across diverse functions and teams. Demonstrated ability to manage senior stakeholders is essential.
Attention to Detail and Perspective: Balances strategic vision with operational precision, ensuring initiatives are both high-impact and well-executed.
Effective Communication: Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to translate complex insights and strategies into clear, compelling narratives.
Resilience and Prioritisation: Skilled in managing multiple priorities in complex, fast-paced environments while maintaining focus on strategic outcomes and member value.
CX & VOC leadership: direct experience in implementing, managing and embedding VOC platforms and CX measurement frameworks within a complex organization. 

Additional Information


Our benefits have been designed so you can tailor your experience with us and include:

Personal and professional development opportunities 
Hybrid working 
Purchase leave scheme and gender neutral 16 weeks paid parental leave 
Super Contribution Continuation for 12 Months of parental leave

About United for Care

United for Care is dedicated to disrupting the disability sector with the purpose of improving quality of life for people living with disability. We are relentless in our pursuit of innovation, inclusion, and human rights, building a new model of care that is person-centred.

Founded in 2020, we have scaled rapidly while maintaining agility. We are now in a critical growth phase that requires robust multi-entity financial oversight, consolidated reporting, and strategic FP&A in a highly regulated environment.

The Opportunity

We are seeking an experienced FP&A professional with proven hands-on experience working across groups of companies (multi-entity structures, consolidations, intercompany eliminations, and group-level reporting). You will play a key role in strengthening financial visibility, forecasting, and decision support across our group entities while partnering closely with the GFC and operational leaders.

This is a hands-on contract role ideal for someone who has thrived in both startup/scale-up settings and larger multi-entity organisations ($50M+ revenue or operating budgets). You will be expected to hit the ground running and deliver immediate value in a dynamic, mission-led environment.

Key Responsibilities

Multi-Entity Financial Planning & Analysis

Lead group-level budgeting, forecasting, and long-range planning across multiple entities.

Prepare consolidated financial reports, including P&L, cash flow, and balance sheet with full intercompany eliminations.

Manage monthly/quarterly variance analysis and driver-based forecasting at both entity and group level.

Business Partnering & Strategic Insights

Provide commercial insights and scenario modelling to support decisions on new developments, service expansions, pricing, and NDIS funding changes.

Develop and maintain KPIs and dashboards (Power BI preferred) that give clear visibility into performance by entity, site, and service line.

Financial Operations & Governance

Own the monthly financial close process from an FP&A perspective, including group consolidations.

Track and analyse capex across entities for property and technology investments.

Identify cost optimisation and efficiency opportunities while protecting care quality.

Support audit readiness, NDIS compliance, and funding submissions with accurate multi-entity analysis.

Process & System Improvement

Review and strengthen multi-entity reporting processes and controls.

Drive automation initiatives to improve efficiency in forecasting and reporting.

What Success Looks Like

Prepared financial viability models across the business, identified new opportunities, and proactively highlighted process inefficiencies and cost-saving initiatives.

Delivered a robust consolidated FY27 budget and rolling forecasts with clear entity-level and group-level insights.

Implemented improved multi-entity variance reporting and scenario models that reduce forecast variance on key drivers.

Strengthened relationships with the GFC and operational leaders through clear, timely financial insights.

Candidate Profile – Essential Experience

You will have strong, hands-on experience managing FP&A across groups of companies, including:

Working with multiple legal entities, consolidations, and intercompany transactions.

Building and maintaining group-level models and reporting.

Required Background

Proven FP&A experience in both startup/scale-up environments (fast-paced, building from scratch) and larger organisations with $50M+ revenue or operating budgets.

Minimum 7+ years’ relevant FP&A experience, with at least 3 years working in a multi-entity / group structure.

Advanced financial modelling skills (Excel + Power BI or equivalent).

Strong understanding of cash flow, unit economics, and scenario planning in a multi-site or multi-entity setting.

Experience in regulated industries (healthcare, disability, aged care, social enterprise, or similar) is highly advantageous.

NDIS funding knowledge is desirable but not mandatory if you are commercially sharp and a fast learner.

Personal Attributes

Mission-aligned and motivated by social impact.

Excellent communicator – able to simplify complex multi-entity financials for non-finance stakeholders.

Proactive, hands-on, and comfortable operating with ambiguity in a growing organisation.

High integrity and strong attention to detail.

What We Offer

Competitive daily/hourly contract rate (negotiable based on experience).

6-month fixed-term contract with high likelihood of extension or conversion.

Opportunity to make a real impact in a high-growth, values-driven NDIS provider.

Exposure to a complex multi-entity group structure in the disability sector.

United for Care is an equal-opportunity employer and strongly encourages applications from people with disability, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, and candidates from diverse backgrounds.

To apply: Please send your resume and a short cover note (max ½ page) outlining your specific experience working across groups of companies / multiple entities and why you are interested in this interim FP&A role at United for Care.

We are looking for someone who can step in quickly and help strengthen our group financial architecture so we can continue delivering exceptional care at scale.

Salary: No contractor fees / two pay runs per week

This is a hybrid role, with the option to be based in our Brisbane, Melbourne, or Sydney offices. Remote work within Australia will also be considered for the right candidate.

Project Delivery

Partner with stakeholders to gather, analyse and validate business requirements.

Design and document functional specifications aligned with IBM solution capabilities.

Configure, test and support IBM modules to meet business needs.

Collaborate with technical teams to deliver customisations, integrations and enhancements.

Lead workshops, demonstrations and training to support solution adoption.

Develop and maintain documentation, including process flows and user guides.

Support change management and transition activities during project go‑lives.

Contribute to project planning, reporting and governance activities.

AMS Support

Strong understanding of business processes across asset management, finance or supply chain.

What we do

At the core of it, we are a location content, visual analytics  and a software as a service company. Innovation is weaved into our DNA.

Job Description


About the Role

Nearmap is a global leader in providing location intelligence technology operating across North America and ANZ. We are looking for a Senior Commercial Analyst who excels across the disciplines of data, strategy and execution.

Reporting into the Director, GTM Finance (ANZ), this role is pivotal in connecting data-driven insights with commercial decision-making across the ANZ Go-to-Market (GTM) function. You will translate complex analysis into clear recommendations that shape strategy, enable operational efficiency and support execution at scale.

Working cross-functionally with key ANZ stakeholders — including Sales and Customer Success leadership — you will develop a deep understanding of the strategic, operational and financial drivers of the Australian business, research and analyse emerging trends and performance outcomes, identify improvement opportunities, and take a hands-on approach to validating and implementing solutions.

The focus of the role is to strengthen the analytical foundation of the ANZ GTM function, improving visibility of revenue performance drivers, enhancing performance reporting, and where applicable, supporting the wider Global Commercial Finance and RevOps teams in refining GTM systems and processes. You will play a critical role in shaping the operational landscape, supporting month-end insights, and ensuring our ANZ GTM teams are empowered with timely, accurate and actionable data.

What You'll Do

Revenue Reporting, Forecasting and Analysis

Attend and contribute to weekly ANZ forecast calls with Sales and Customer Success leaders, interpreting and sense-checking pipeline and revenue numbers.
Contribute to the weekly Flash Report and other regular reporting cycles.
Understand and interpret key ANZ metrics including new bookings, churn, downgrade, retention and price increase performance.
Together with the data team, design and maintain dashboards and visual reporting templates to surface financial performance trends, patterns and insights to support ANZ GTM leadership decision-making.
Partner with senior management to enable data-driven decisions, identifying data requirements across market analysis, planning, monitoring, financial modelling, pricing and reporting.
Support the Director, GTM Finance (ANZ) by contributing to budgeting, forecasting and reporting for consolidation.

Commission & Month-End Support

Provide ANZ-specific context and input into commission calculations and month-end processes, partnering with the global Commercial Finance team to ensure accuracy and local relevance.
Interpret ANZ-specific commission structures and edge cases, flagging issues and opportunities as they arise.

Strategic Analysis

Deliver analysis and insights on key strategic issues on an ad-hoc basis, including market intelligence, competitive landscape, product and pricing optimisation and growth opportunities.
Partner with GTM, Product and Finance teams to support strategic planning, investment decisions and commercial growth initiatives.
Build business cases and scenario models to assess new markets, partnerships and GTM strategies.

Stakeholder Engagement & Reporting

Own key stakeholder relationships with ANZ GTM leadership, supporting business case development and KPI reporting.
Deliver insights and recommendations that inform strategic and operational decision-making across the ANZ GTM organisation.

Process Evaluation & Improvement

Working alongside the RevOps team, assess, evaluate, and optimise business processes, anticipating requirements, identifying gaps and developing solutions that drive efficiency, accuracy and scalability.

Global Collaboration

Build and maintain strong relationships with global business partners, ensuring consistent understanding of data flows, inputs and outputs from multiple systems.
Act as a bridge between ANZ and global teams to standardise reporting and analytical processes.
Mentor and coach junior staff members within the team where applicable.

Qualifications


Experience

5+ years' experience in commercial finance, business analytics or commercial operations — ideally within a SaaS or technology business.
Go-to-market or commercial finance experience is highly regarded — familiarity with pipeline, renewals, commissions, bookings and churn metrics is a strong advantage.

Skills

Strong commercial acumen with deep analytical skills.
Lateral and critical thinking; ability to self-start and execute on non-process driven outcomes.
Advanced Excel skills with the ability to build and manage complex financial models.
Proven ability to influence decision-makers and present data-driven insights with clarity and impact.
Experience in revenue forecasting, financial analysis, and a solid understanding of KPIs and commercial drivers.
Hands-on experience with BI, data warehousing, statistical or reporting tools.
Experience with FP&A tools such as Adaptive, TM1 or Dynamics preferred.
Working knowledge of CRM systems; Salesforce experience is advantageous.
Familiarity with SQL, Tableau or similar analytics and visualisation platforms preferred.

Personal Attributes

Hands-on self-starter with strong analytical and conceptual thinking skills.
Comfortable operating outside of standardised procedures in a fast-moving environment.
Growth-oriented mindset with a drive to continuously improve and adapt.
Able to read a room — knows when something needs to be escalated and acts on it.
Excellent communication skills with the ability to present complex financial topics clearly to non-finance stakeholders.
Proven ability to manage competing priorities and deliver to deadlines.

Education

Relevant tertiary qualifications in business or a related field.
Financial qualification favourable but not essential.

Additional Information


Working at Nearmap
We move fast and work smart; often wearing multiple hats. We adapted to remote working with ease and are continually looking at ways to improve. We’re proud of our inclusive, supportive culture, and maintain a safe environment where everyone feels a sense of belonging and can be themselves.

If you can see yourself working at Nearmap and feel you have the right level of experience, we invite you to get in touch. 

Watch our culture video below to find out more about what a day in the life at Nearmap looks like.

Nearmap does not accept unsolicited resumes from recruitment agencies and search firms. Please do not email or send unsolicited resumes to any Nearmap employee, location or address. Nearmap is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

Product Manager – InsurTech Scale-Up

Location: Australia - Flexible
Type: Full-time
Travel: Occasional customer travel across Australia

Australian work authorisation is required for this position.

About Sync Technologies

Insurance decisions today are often made on fragmented information with little visual context. SyncTech changes that.

Our platform combines visual-first workflows, digital twins, AI-driven damage detection, and remote inspection technology to help insurers, builders, and assessors make faster, clearer, and more defensible decisions.

We are past early product–market fit and already working with major enterprise insurers in Australia and Europe. Now we are entering the next phase of our journey: scaling adoption, expanding product capabilities, and building the category leader in visual property intelligence.

We are building a company for the long term — and looking for people who want to help shape it.







The role

We are looking for a Product Manager who is curious, driven, and excited to build meaningful products in a fast-moving environment.

This is not a maintenance role. You will help shape the future of our product.

You will work closely with customers, engineers, and leadership to understand real-world problems and translate them into products that create tangible value.

You'll join at a pivotal moment for SyncTech — when the product is proven, customers are scaling, and the decisions we make now will shape the platform for years to come.

If you enjoy solving complex problems, getting close to customers, and building products that people rely on in the real world, you'll thrive here.







What you'll own

Product strategy & roadmap

Help define and prioritise product initiatives across key areas of the SyncTech platform

Contribute to shaping the product roadmap together with the CPO and engineering team

Define success metrics and help track outcomes against product goals

Discovery & customer research

Work directly with customers including claims professionals, assessors, and insurers to understand workflows and pain points

Run user interviews, product discovery sessions, and usability testing

Identify opportunities where technology can simplify complex real-world processes

Help translate insights into product ideas and experiments

Delivery & execution

Partner closely with engineering and design to deliver product improvements and new capabilities

Write clear product requirements and maintain a well-prioritised backlog

Participate in sprint planning, reviews, and retrospectives

Ensure product decisions remain grounded in customer outcomes

Cross-team collaboration

Work closely with engineering, design, operations, and commercial teams

Support internal teams in understanding product capabilities and customer use cases

Contribute to building strong product practices as the SyncTech product team grows

What you bring

We care more about how you think and how you work than about ticking every box.

3–6+ years of experience working in product management or a similar product-focused role

This role is suitable for both mid-level and senior product management professionals.

Experience working with engineers and designers to ship real products

Strong curiosity about users and the problems they face

The ability to break complex problems into clear, structured solutions

Excellent communication skills and the ability to collaborate across teams

A strong sense of ownership — you like taking responsibility and driving things forward

Most importantly, you are someone who:

enjoys learning quickly in new domains

is comfortable with ambiguity and pace

is motivated by building something meaningful from the ground up

Helpful but not required

Experience in insurtech, proptech, or workflow-heavy industries

Exposure to AI/ML-driven products or data platforms

Experience working in startups or scale-ups

Familiarity with geospatial, visual data, or 3D capture technologies







Why join SyncTech?

Real impact

Our technology helps insurers make better decisions when real homes and buildings are damaged.

Build something meaningful

You'll work on products that solve real-world problems and are used by industry professionals every day.

High ownership

You won't inherit a decade of legacy systems. You'll help shape what we build next.

Learn fast

You'll work closely with experienced founders, engineers, and customers in a fast-learning environment.

Grow with the company

As SyncTech grows, the product organisation will grow with it.







Interested?

If you're excited about building great products, learning quickly, and helping shape the future of a fast-growing InsurTech, we'd love to hear from you.

Your Team

The Risk Transformation team is part of CFS's Legal, Governance, Risk and Compliance Business Unit which is accountable for analysis and strategic advisory, oversight and respectful challenge of the business along with driving the commercial application of the Risk Management Policy, tools, processes and systems. The Risk Transformation team is currently establishing a risk transformation project, aimed at further optimising risk management through a range of digitisation and automation initiatives such as continuous control monitoring, control testing automation and integrated risk assessments. You will have the opportunity to be part of the CFS's risk transformation journey from the beginning. We are aiming to establish an integrated project team comprising of contingent risk SMEs like yourself and our existing line 1 and line 2 risk SMEs, co-delivering the future of risk at CFS.

Your Responsibilities

Design and deliver end‑ to‑ end risk solutions that align with business strategy, using technology, automation, and process enhancements to drive uplift with pace and accountability.
Use critical thinking, strong problem solving and data led insight to design the future state of Risk and Control Self Assessments (RCSAs), Risk Matters, Obligations, KRIs, and control testing outcomes backed by deep analysis.
Conduct complex business analysis to diagnose problem areas, identify improvement opportunities, and resolve misalignments in operating practices through structured analysis and process mapping.
Apply a good understanding of regulatory requirements (e.g. licensing, conduct, privacy, financial crime) to ensure solution designs and delivery outcomes remain compliant and aligned to industry obligations.
Partner with Line 1 and Line 2 risk and compliance team stakeholders to embed and adopt risk practices that align to CFS Frameworks, supporting sustainable change and driving adoption.
Engagement with stakeholders by facilitating workshops, eliciting and balancing conflicting requirements, documenting clear current‑ and future‑ state processes that trace directly to Work Stream Scope whilst maintaining progress against the critical path.
Design fit for purpose risk and compliance frameworks that that meet regulatory expectations and align with risk appetite, ensuring practical, scalable, and outcome focused delivery that consider impacts to users and sustainable adoption.
Support the delivery of a scalable, centralised control testing capability, including standardised testing templates, partial or fully automated testing, and the use of operational and system metadata to enable continuous assurance.
Enable and contribute an ethical risk culture across CFS Group by encouraging transparency, accountability, applying a solution‑ oriented mindset.

Your Capability and Experience

Significant years of experience in risk and compliance advisory, ideally in financial services or other highly regulated environments, with the ability to translate complex risk problems into practical delivery outcomes.
Experience in project or transformation delivery, demonstrating an end‑to‑end mindset and the ability to deliver with pace, energy and accountability.
Tertiary qualifications or equivalent experience supporting strong professional judgement and analytical discipline.
Highly developed analytical, troubleshooting and problem‑solving capability, to think quickly diagnose issues and shape clear, actionable solutions supported by deep analysis.
Be curious and have a growth-oriented mindset, actively seeking new ways of working, emerging technologies and innovative approaches that make a positive difference.
Strong business analysis and delivery capability, including structured problem solving, requirements translation, and process mapping/design.
Excellent prioritisation and execution skills, able to manage competing priorities effectively in fast‑ paced environments.
Proven ability to influence and collaborate at senior levels, confidently engaging executives and working effectively across teams, functions, and operating models.

What to Expect

At CFS, you'll be working among the very best in the wealth management industry. It's an inspiring environment that encourages development and celebrates success. Other things to look forward to:

Hybrid working model

Access to CFS Employer Super

Access to Sonder who provides human-powered medical, mental health and safety support through one easy-to-use app enabling you to get support whenever and wherever you need.

Access to Corporate rates with BUPA Health Insurance
Confidential coaching and counselling services supporting life, mind, body, relationships, work and family to assist you in taking charge of your own health and wellbeing


CFS Culture

At CFS we are committed to creating a thriving environment where individuals can flourish. We believe that success is built upon strong teams, and we are dedicated to celebrating uniqueness, championing individuality and supporting a diverse and inclusive workforce. We believe that when you can truly be yourself, you can unlock your full potential.

Apply today and join us in helping Australians to achieve their financial freedom.

Please note, CFS requires all candidates to have full work rights in Australia.

Where we have preferred candidates, background checks (including Police, Employment, Bankruptcy checks, ASIC banned and disqualified persons) will be completed prior to the final preferred candidate's employment being confirmed. The outcomes of the background checks do not preclude the preferred candidate, however, they will be assessed against the inherent requirements of the role.

About the Business:

Progress Rail is a wholly owned subsidiary of Caterpillar Inc. and a leading provider of rolling stock, infrastructure and advanced technologies for global rail customers. The company delivers EMD® locomotives and engines, trackwork, signaling, fasteners, and comprehensive locomotive and freight car services, along with aftermarket parts and recycling. About VIDA ROADS

VIDA Roads is delivering a massive investment in the state's roads. We're delivering major new road projects across metropolitan Melbourne and regional Victoria while improving the roads people rely on every day.

The West Gate Tunnel Project will give Melbourne a second freeway link between the west and the city - providing a much-needed alternative to the West Gate Bridge and a vital second river crossing.

Northeast Link will fix the missing link in Melbourne's freeway network with new 6.5 km tunnels from Watsonia to Bulleen. We're also overhauling the Eastern Freeway and completing the M80 Ring Road at Greensborough.

Road Programs are also delivering dozens of new roads, bridges, road widening projects and major freeway upgrades.

Come and be a part of our centre of roads excellence.

Job Title: Data Product Engineer (BI & AI Data Products)
Ad Text:
At our core, RSL LifeCare provides care and services to veterans and seniors who live in our communities or are receiving support at home. Our vision of creating proud communities living their best lives is at the heart of everything we do as we continue to build upon our legacy of supporting veterans and their families for more than 100 years.

This is an opportunity for you to join a large social purpose organisation where your skills and compassion will be valued and enable you to make a meaningful difference to people's lives.

We're looking for an experienced Data Product Engineer to help shape how we use data across the organisation.

In this role, you'll design, build, and maintain reliable, reusable data products that support everything from day‑to‑day reporting to advanced analytics and AI.
You'll take the lead on our curated data layer, semantic models, and enterprise KPI definitions-making sure our data is consistent, high‑quality, and easy for the business to use.
Your work will play a key part in getting the organisation ready for AI and helping us scale our analytics capability in a safe and efficient way.
You'll also collaborate closely with the BI Developer, Data Engineer and BI Developer - Insights Partner, working together to build a strong, resilient team and a data environment the whole organisation can rely on.

You will have:

Bachelor's degree in computer science, information systems, data analytics, or related field.
Strong experience in dimensional modelling (Kimball) and enterprise semantic model design.
Advanced Power BI semantic modelling skills, including DAX, KPI/measure design, and performance optimisation.
Hands‑on experience delivering curated analytics datasets using Microsoft Fabric (Warehouse/Lakehouse, Notebooks, Pipelines).
Strong SQL skills and experience implementing business transformation logic.
Experience defining and applying data quality rules for analytics and reporting.
Exposure to supporting self‑service BI through certified datasets and semantic model reuse.
Exposure to preparing datasets for AI and advanced analytics.
Ability to translate business concepts into reusable, well‑governed data products.

Desirable

Certifications in Power BI, Microsoft Fabric, or data analytics platforms.
Strong Python skills for data science use cases.

Job Title: Manager, Systems Engineering, and Software Business Analysis
Ad Text:
Cochlear's mission is to help people hear and be heard. As the global leader in implantable hearing solutions, Cochlear is dedicated to helping people with moderate to profound hearing loss experience a world full of sound. We aim to transform the way people understand and treat hearing loss and innovate to connect people with future technologies. Our employees tell us that the number one reason they enjoy working for Cochlear is the opportunity to make a difference to people's lives. Learn and grow with us as we tackle the most complex challenges in helping more people to experience a lifetime of hearing.

The Opportunity

The Manager, Software Systems Engineering & Business Analysis is a senior leadership role responsible for defining and governing system behaviour across Cochlear's complex medical device software ecosystem. You will lead multidisciplinary teams spanning Systems Engineering, Software Business Analysis and System Verification & Validation, ensuring safe, compliant and clinically effective system delivery.

Operating at the intersection of clinical intent, system architecture and regulated delivery, this role provides both people leadership and deep technical authority. You will influence system-level decisions across customer software, workflow engines, algorithmic modules, firmware integration and device communication layers, while building discipline maturity and engineering excellence across the organisation.

Responsibilities include:

Lead and develop multidisciplinary teams across Systems Engineering, Software Business Analysis and System V&V, building capability, engagement and succession

Define and govern system‑level behaviour, ensuring requirements are clear, traceable, verifiable and aligned to clinical intent

Establish and maintain end‑to‑end traceability across user needs, system requirements, interfaces and verification evidence

Lead system‑level technical decision‑making, risk management and issue resolution in complex, safety‑critical environments

Ensure compliance with medical device regulatory and quality standards including ISO 13485, ISO 14971, IEC 62304, IEC 60601 and FDA requirements

Sponsor and chair system‑level design reviews, ensuring architectural alignment, integration readiness and verification depth

Act as a primary interface between architecture functions and delivery teams to ensure effective system implementation

Champion systems thinking, discipline excellence and continuous improvement through guild leadership, process ownership and tool evolution

About You.

As we grow our operations, we are looking for people who share our passion delivering safe, effective, and high-quality hearing solutions. To add value to Cochlear in this role, you'll be able to demonstrate the following skills and experience:

Extensive experience leading multidisciplinary R&D teams in regulated medical device environments

Deep expertise in software‑centric systems engineering, requirements engineering and system‑level verification

Strong working knowledge of medical device regulatory and quality frameworks (ISO 13485, ISO 14971, IEC 62304, IEC 60601, FDA)

Proven ability to define, govern and verify complex system behaviour across software, firmware and hardware interfaces

Excellent decision‑making and risk management capability in ambiguous, safety‑critical contexts

Highly effective communication and stakeholder influence skills across technical, clinical and executive audiences

What Would Make You Stand Out

Experience leading system behaviour definition for workflow‑driven software, parameter‑driven algorithms or real‑time communication systems, along with a track record of building discipline maturity through standards, tooling and engineering process improvement.

Why this role is for you?

This role offers the opportunity to operate as both a people leader and technical authority in one of Cochlear's most complex engineering domains. You'll shape system behaviour that enables safe, reliable clinical outcomes, influence future platform evolution, and build high‑performing teams whose work directly changes lives.

If you feel that you have the skills and experience to be successful in this role and take on new challenges to build your career with Cochlear, please start your application by clicking the apply button below.

How we recognise your contribution

At Cochlear we value and welcome the unique contributions, perspectives, experiences, and backgrounds of our employees and aim to build a culture that celebrates and leverages these differences, creating a sense of belonging and enabling our people to realise their full potential. Through our internal programs and employee benefits, we aim to create an environment where our people will feel value and supported. Whether your focus is on continuous learning, professional development or simply finding an environment which enables you to thrive whilst balancing family or personal life commitments, then we have several programs in place to support you.

For more information about Life at Cochlear, visit www.cochlearcareers.com

Salary: Half a day each Wednesday! Hybrid working.
Ad Text:
Company Description

Fairness feels good 

Make a real impact at AFCA. Where fairness drives every decision. Help us deliver world-class, independent complaints resolution for Australians. As a not-for-profit and progressive financial ombudsman, we’re championing positive change. Achieving our purpose takes progressive thinking, accountability and resilience. At AFCA, our inclusive leadership values every voice. We offer our people flexible work options, thoughtful benefits and opportunities to deepen expertise. Flourish in a diverse, caring culture. Feel the difference of belonging to an organisation intentionally designed to put people first. 

Job Description

AFCA is undertaking a significant expansion of its remit through the Scams External Dispute Resolution (EDR) Project, a critical initiative that will shape how Australians are protected in an increasingly complex financial environment.

We’re looking for a Finance Business Partner to play a pivotal role in designing, influencing and implementing financial change for this landmark program. This is a high-impact opportunity for a commercially astute finance professional who thrives at the intersection of strategy, modelling and stakeholder partnership.

As AFCA’s Finance Business Partner – Scams EDR, you will be a key advisor to senior leaders, working closely with the Scams EDR project team, the broader Finance function, and cross‑functional stakeholders across the organisation.

You’ll lead financial reporting and modelling for the project, support the review and implementation of AFCA’s funding model, and ensure financial integrity as the Scams EDR transitions from design into delivery. This role sits within Corporate Services and reports to the Senior Manager – Finance Business Partnering & Transformation.

What you’ll be doing

In this strategically focused role, you will:

Lead project financial reporting, including grant agreement obligations
Act as the primary point of contact for all Scams EDR finance-related queries
Build and maintain driver‑based financial models covering revenue, costs, capital expenditure and cash flow
Conduct sensitivity analysis and scenario modelling to support executive decision making
Integrate financial model outputs into AFCA’s annual budget processes for the Scams EDR program and operations
Support the review, approval and implementation of changes to AFCA’s funding model in the context of Scams EDR
Represent AFCA in responding to external stakeholder and funding model queries
Collaborate closely with internal stakeholders to develop recommendations and implementation approaches
Manage vendor engagements, systems testing and validation related to funding model and financial system changes
Partner with internal IT, data teams, auditors and third‑party suppliers to ensure robust, end‑to‑end delivery

This is an initial 18-month contract role.

Qualifications

What you’ll bring

You’re a confident finance partner with strong technical capability and the presence to influence at senior levels. You’ll also bring:

Tertiary qualifications in Accounting, Finance, Economics or a related field
CA or CPA qualification
several years’ experience in financial analysis, commercial finance or business partnering roles
Advanced financial modelling capability, including driver‑based models and scenario analysis
Strong systems expertise (e.g. Microsoft D365 FinOps, SAP, Oracle) and advanced Excel skills
Exceptional analytical skills, attention to detail and a high level of numeracy
Clear, persuasive communication skills with the ability to translate complex concepts for diverse audiences
Proven ability to manage competing priorities and deliver under pressure
A collaborative mindset and experience working with cross‑functional stakeholders
High ethical standards and a strong commitment to confidentiality
Additional Information

Additional Information

BOSS Best Places to Work 2024 –credited for its culture, engagement and flexible working arrangements. 
Most Inclusive Workplace 2024 – Australian HR Institute (AHRI) Awards. 
Employer of Choice Public Sector and NFP – Australian HR Awards 2023 
Bronze AWEI Accreditation 2024 – Recognised for LGBTQ+ workplace inclusion. 
Accredited Family Friendly Workplace – Supporting work-life balance and inclusivity. 
Hybrid working – Flexible arrangements with state-of-the-art offices designed for collaboration and wellbeing. 
Inclusive leave options – Flexible public holidays, 20 weeks paid parental leave, gender affirmation leave, women’s health leave, and paid time off over Christmas. 
Locations – A team of over 1,200 dedicated professionals based in modern Melbourne and Sydney CBD offices. 

To apply 

If you’re passionate about fairness and believe your skills align with this role, we encourage you to apply—even if you don’t meet every single criterion. 

We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, cultures, abilities, sexual orientations, and gender identities. If you require any accessibility support during the recruitment process, please reach out to our team at Careers@afca.org.au. 

AFCA is a 2025 Circle Back Initiative Employer - we are committed to responding to every applicant.

A unique opportunity to join a key leading regulator in the energy sector for the Victorian State Government.

Are you a senior portfolio or change leader who thrives at the intersection of strategy, regulation and delivery? Do you bring the confidence to challenge, influence and support executives while maintaining a practical, hands‑on approach?



Your new company

This organisation is a trusted statutory authority responsible for regulating energy safety, supply and efficiency across Victoria. Operating in a complex and highly scrutinised environment, it plays a critical role in ensuring energy is used safely, responsibly and with confidence.

Your new role
Reporting to the Head of Strategy and Risk, the Portfolio Manager plays a pivotal enterprise role in shaping how strategy is mobilised and delivered across the organisation.
This is not an IT or technical portfolio role. It is focused on regulatory, operational and organisational change, providing authoritative oversight of a complex portfolio.

Key responsibilities include:

Providing enterprise‑level stewardship of the portfolio, ensuring the organisation is investing in the right priorities at the right time.
Translating strategic intent into well‑defined, delivery‑ready programs and initiatives.
Supporting regulators and business owners through periods of regulatory and operational change.
Leading portfolio‑level prioritisation, sequencing and trade‑off analysis to balance demand, risk and organisational capacity.
Driving performance, delivery confidence and risk mitigation across the portfolio.
Preparing high‑quality, evidence‑based insights and advice for Executive and governance forums.
Leading and developing a small, diverse team (2 direct reports and potential contractors), fostering inclusive, high‑performance ways of working.
Uplifting portfolio and change capability, frameworks and internal processes across the organisation.
This role requires strong judgement, comfort with ambiguity, and the ability to move seamlessly between strategic advisory and hands‑on leadership.



What you'll need to succeed

Senior experience leading enterprise portfolios or complex change, with credibility advising executives on prioritisation, sequencing and investment trade‑offs.
Proven ability to mobilise strategy, shaping initiatives from concept through to delivery readiness within regulated or complex environments.
Strong executive presence, with sound judgement and the confidence to provide clear, constructive challenge.
Excellent analytical and communication skills, translating complex portfolio, risk and delivery information into actionable insights.
A hands‑on people leader, experienced in leading small, diverse teams and improving performance.
A pragmatic change leadership mindset, supporting leaders through regulatory and operational change.
Sound financial and investment acumen, including business cases, benefits and risk management.
Consulting or advisory background is highly regarded.


What you'll get in return

Permanent ongoing role with a salary of $163,420 - $178,952 + Super
A senior, influential leadership role within a respected Victorian Government regulator.
The opportunity to shape how enterprise change is prioritised, governed and delivered.
Meaningful work that contributes to public safety and regulatory confidence.
Supportive executive leadership, flexibility, and a values‑driven culture.
Ongoing professional growth in a complex, purpose‑led organisation.


What you need to do now

If you're ready to step into a pivotal Portfolio Manager role where strategy, regulation and delivery intersect, click Apply Now or contact parminder.kaur@hays.com.au for a confidential discussion.

If this isn't quite the right role, but you're exploring a senior portfolio or change leadership opportunities, feel free to reach out. And if you know someone who would be a strong fit, please share this opportunity.

Frame strategic problems, lead research and analysis, and develop evidence-based options and recommendations that guide investment decisions and priorities.

Build and maintain deep competitor and market understanding, identifying growth opportunities, risks and emerging threats.

Varied, high-impact work: tackle complex, enterprise-wide problems and opportunities that shape Sime's future direction.

Executive engagement and participation in high-impact governance and decision forums.

Join a dynamic team known for high standards, strong thinking, and trusted partnerships across the organisation.

Our Values:
At Sime, we're guided by our values: Act with Integrity, Care for All and Win Together.

a complex strategic problem you've worked on and the approach you took, and an example of influencing senior stakeholders through a clear recommendation and rationale.

Working at the heart of a complex, high-volume distribution environment, you'll partner closely with operational leaders to drive performance, unlock efficiencies, and influence key strategic decisions that shape the future of the business.

About the Opportunity

Sitting within a large, high-performing finance function, you'll act as a true business partner to distribution centre operations, providing critical insights into financial and operational performance across a national network.

You'll play a key role in translating data into action, helping senior stakeholders understand performance drivers, identify opportunities, and execute initiatives that improve productivity, cost efficiency, and long-term value creation.

Partner with operational and finance stakeholders to drive performance across distribution centre operations
Deliver weekly, monthly and ad hoc reporting on financial and operational KPIs
Provide clear, actionable insights into cost drivers, risks and opportunities
Influence decision-making across areas such as productivity, workforce planning and continuous improvement initiatives
Lead or contribute to national working groups and transformation initiatives
Support budgeting, forecasting and financial planning cycles
Collaborate on major capital projects, including business case development, investment analysis and post-implementation reviews
Engage cross-functionally with teams such as procurement, workforce relations and project delivery to optimise outcomes

We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Data Engineer to join our Data and Insights. This role is critical in the ongoing administration, enhancement, and optimisation of our enterprise data platforms. People First Bank (PFB) currently operates on-premise data warehouses and is progressively building a modern Data and Insights capability

About the role 

We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Data Engineer to join our Data and Insights team in a 12 months Fixed Term Contract. This role is critical in the ongoing administration, enhancement, and optimisation of our enterprise data platforms. People First Bank (PFB) currently operates on-premise data warehouses and is progressively building a modern Data and Insights capability.

This role is offered as a 12 months Fixed Term Contract with potential permanency or extensions.

Key Responsibilities:

Liaise with business departments to evaluate and determine data needs and requirements. Apply technical and analytical skills to solutions to improve PFB  reporting, advanced analytics, and data insights.

Work with Business Intelligence Analysts, Data Base Administrators, Business Analysts, IT and other key stakeholders to, maintain, organise and improve data warehouse solutions. Assist in the development of future business intelligence strategy and data governance decisions for PFB.

Maintain effective commercial relationships with external service providers to deliver optimal data management and reporting outcomes for the business.

Engineer & model curated Data Warehouse solutions to support BI objectives and timeframes in relation to the development of key analytics and metrics 

Partner with technical teams to build maturity and capability in relation to implementing data capture, ETL, validation, and test methods

Participate in an on-call support roster to help monitor, maintain, and troubleshoot the Data and Insights platform, ensuring high availability and reliability of critical data services.

Maintain and assist in data model documentation, data dictionary, data flow, data mapping and other MDM and Data Governance documentation




About you 

You are the go-to experts in crafting, implementing, and sustaining resilient data processing pipelines. Your role is pivotal in guaranteeing the effectiveness, precision, and scalability of our data infrastructure, impacting not only the Data and Insights team but also extending across the entire organization.

Knowledge and Experience:

Extensive experience in a similar role within a complex data environment.

Strong hands-on experience with ETL tools, preferably SSIS and WhereScape.

Advanced SQL skills, including the ability to write complex queries using sub-queries, nested queries, and other advanced SQL techniques.

Proficient in the Microsoft BI stack, including SSAS, SSRS, and SSIS, with additional exposure to Power BI for reporting and dashboarding.

Experience with CDC (Change Data Capture) tools, specifically Precisely, for real-time and incremental data movement.

Experience working with Azure DevOps, including Git repositories and Azure Pipelines; familiarity with PowerShell scripting and Power BI deployment.

Solid understanding of enterprise data warehouse (EDW) methodologies, including data analysis, data modelling, data integration, and data warehouse design.




Who are we? 

It's an exciting time to join our organisation! On 1 March 2023, two of Australia's most successful member-owned banking organisations merged to form People First Bank – powered by Heritage and People's Choice.  We are Australia's leading mutual banking organisation and a genuine, member-owned alternative to the major banks. 

We exist to create a better future for our members and the communities in which they live. 

We are 100% member owned, with approximately 720,000 members and 1,900 employees. We have dual head offices in Adelaide and Toowoomba, and 95 branches across South Australia, Victoria, New South Wales, Queensland and the Northern Territory, trading under two brands, Heritage Bank and People's Choice. 

When you come to work with us, you'll be joining a team of inclusive, friendly, and motivated employees who value making a difference every day for our members, community, and the planet. We are committed to creating an outstanding working environment where you feel supported, can continue to develop and are proud advocates of our members and our business.  




Benefits of working for us  

We believe the little extras can make a big difference in supporting your success. That's why we offer great incentives and benefits including: 

Extensive range of financial and lifestyle benefits, including (but not limited to): 

Paid parental leave and special leave provisions 

Flexibility and hybrid working arrangements

Employee banking benefits and discounted insurances 

Career development opportunities, and ongoing training, coaching and support 

A dedicated Employee Assistance Program for you and your family to access in times of need 

Paid volunteering days and access to a diverse range of community and charitable initiatives 




If you are ready to join a supportive team who are passionate about the work they do and the results they achieve, then apply now.

If this sounds like you, then this is the next step in your career. Click 'Apply' now and submit your application, including a cover letter and current resume by 13th April 2026

We are committed to diversity and inclusion and support candidate requests for adjustments to enable everyone to equitably participate in our selection process.

About the role 

We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Data Engineer to join our Data and Insights team in a 12 months Fixed Term Contract. This role is critical in the ongoing administration, enhancement, and optimisation of our enterprise data platforms. People First Bank (PFB) currently operates on-premise data warehouses and is progressively building a modern Data and Insights capability.

This role is offered as a 12 months Fixed Term Contract with potential permanency or extensions.

Key Responsibilities:

Liaise with business departments to evaluate and determine data needs and requirements. Apply technical and analytical skills to solutions to improve PFB  reporting, advanced analytics, and data insights.

Work with Business Intelligence Analysts, Data Base Administrators, Business Analysts, IT and other key stakeholders to, maintain, organise and improve data warehouse solutions. Assist in the development of future business intelligence strategy and data governance decisions for PFB.

Maintain effective commercial relationships with external service providers to deliver optimal data management and reporting outcomes for the business.

Engineer & model curated Data Warehouse solutions to support BI objectives and timeframes in relation to the development of key analytics and metrics 

Partner with technical teams to build maturity and capability in relation to implementing data capture, ETL, validation, and test methods

Participate in an on-call support roster to help monitor, maintain, and troubleshoot the Data and Insights platform, ensuring high availability and reliability of critical data services.

Maintain and assist in data model documentation, data dictionary, data flow, data mapping and other MDM and Data Governance documentation




About you 

You are the go-to experts in crafting, implementing, and sustaining resilient data processing pipelines. Your role is pivotal in guaranteeing the effectiveness, precision, and scalability of our data infrastructure, impacting not only the Data and Insights team but also extending across the entire organization.

Knowledge and Experience:

Extensive experience in a similar role within a complex data environment.

Strong hands-on experience with ETL tools, preferably SSIS and WhereScape.

Advanced SQL skills, including the ability to write complex queries using sub-queries, nested queries, and other advanced SQL techniques.

Proficient in the Microsoft BI stack, including SSAS, SSRS, and SSIS, with additional exposure to Power BI for reporting and dashboarding.

Experience with CDC (Change Data Capture) tools, specifically Precisely, for real-time and incremental data movement.

Experience working with Azure DevOps, including Git repositories and Azure Pipelines; familiarity with PowerShell scripting and Power BI deployment.

Solid understanding of enterprise data warehouse (EDW) methodologies, including data analysis, data modelling, data integration, and data warehouse design.




Who are we? 

It's an exciting time to join our organisation! On 1 March 2023, two of Australia's most successful member-owned banking organisations merged to form People First Bank – powered by Heritage and People's Choice.  We are Australia's leading mutual banking organisation and a genuine, member-owned alternative to the major banks. 

We exist to create a better future for our members and the communities in which they live. 

We are 100% member owned, with approximately 720,000 members and 1,900 employees. We have dual head offices in Adelaide and Toowoomba, and 95 branches across South Australia, Victoria, New South Wales, Queensland and the Northern Territory, trading under two brands, Heritage Bank and People's Choice. 

When you come to work with us, you'll be joining a team of inclusive, friendly, and motivated employees who value making a difference every day for our members, community, and the planet. We are committed to creating an outstanding working environment where you feel supported, can continue to develop and are proud advocates of our members and our business.  




Benefits of working for us  

We believe the little extras can make a big difference in supporting your success. That's why we offer great incentives and benefits including: 

Extensive range of financial and lifestyle benefits, including (but not limited to): 

Paid parental leave and special leave provisions 

Flexibility and hybrid working arrangements

Employee banking benefits and discounted insurances 

Career development opportunities, and ongoing training, coaching and support 

A dedicated Employee Assistance Program for you and your family to access in times of need 

Paid volunteering days and access to a diverse range of community and charitable initiatives 




If you are ready to join a supportive team who are passionate about the work they do and the results they achieve, then apply now.

If this sounds like you, then this is the next step in your career. Click 'Apply' now and submit your application, including a cover letter and current resume by 13th April 2026

We are committed to diversity and inclusion and support candidate requests for adjustments to enable everyone to equitably participate in our selection process.

Job Title: Reporting Manager - Talent Acquisition Reports, Enterprise Account
Ad Text:
Reporting Manager – Enterprise/Financial Services, TA Reports (Sydney)

As the Reporting Manager, you will join a high-performing Hudson Talent Acquisition team, focusing on supporting one of our clients in the Enterprise/Financial Services sector. Your primary responsibility will be to understand and analyze recruitment processes to enhance information management and support strategic decision-making within the TA teams and client Human Resources functions.

What you’ll do:
 
Operate as a dedicated resource for an enterprise RPO account, ensuring reporting and analytics activities align with client priorities, service level agreements, and overall business objectives.
Oversee data governance and ensure compliance with organizational standards across Avature and BI systems.
Lead the design and implementation of reporting strategies and analytics projects from conception to delivery.
Drive innovation by introducing automation, AI, and Power Platform solutions to optimize reporting processes.
Support the creation of budgets in collaboration with senior stakeholders by providing accurate reporting and financial insights to guide resource allocation and strategic decisions.
Provide leadership and mentoring to the reporting team, fostering a culture of continuous improvement.
Collaborate with senior management on strategic initiatives and present insights to inform decision-making.
Manage stakeholder relationships to ensure reporting aligns with business objectives.

What You Will Bring to the Role:
 
Typically requires at least 5 years of experience in reporting or data analysis roles, with RPO experience strongly preferred.
Proficiency in Excel and BI tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau).
Experience with ATS/CRM/HRIS systems (e.g., Workday, SuccessFactors, Avature, Smart Recruiters), with implementation in this space highly sought after.
Strong financial acumen with the ability to interpret and analyze budget data, ensuring alignment between reporting outputs and financial objectives.
Proven leadership and guidance skills, mentoring junior analysts and fostering a culture of continuous improvement.
Champion innovation by driving the adoption of emerging technologies such as AI, automation, and Microsoft Power Platform (including Power Apps) to optimize reporting strategies and enhance team capabilities.
Demonstrated experience working closely with management or leadership teams, including exposure to decision-making processes and strategic-level reporting.
Experience in managing reporting and analytics projects from conception to implementation.
Excellent relationship management skills, with effective communication ability with stakeholders at all levels of the organization.

What Does Great Look Like in This Role:
 
Proactively identifies challenges and opportunities for improvement, driving innovation in reporting methodologies.
Acts as a trusted advisor for reporting and analytics within the business.
Contributes to the budgeting process to enhance profitability.
Fosters team success through mentoring and knowledge sharing.
Receives consistent positive feedback from stakeholders.
Serves as a proud ambassador of Hudson Talent Solutions.

Please note that parts of our communication used in the course of providing our services are generated with AI assistance and have been reviewed for accuracy by a human. As a company, we are committed to the responsible and ethical use of AI in our services.

Are you ready to be part of an organisation that values expertise, passion and diversity? At CFS we know that the foundation of our success lies in our exceptional people. We believe in celebrating individuality, have a passion for high performance and creating an environment where you can unleash your full potential. Our people enable us to make a difference and deliver exceptional experiences to help our customers achieve financial freedom.


Your Team

The Digital Product Owner (Adviser) role sits within our Digital & Customer Experience team alongside other Product Owners, UX/UI Designers and content copy writers..

You will develop and execute our digital strategy and product roadmap for our adviser web portal, Firstnet Adviser. Working closely with Product, Technology, Operations, and Distribution stakeholders, you will be accountable for leveraging adviser insights and research to help shape and prioritise the product backlog to ensure we deliver innovative digital solutions for our advisers.

This role is a 12 Month Maximum term contract in Sydney CBD (Hybrid working).


Your Responsibilities

Lead the end-to-end delivery of the product roadmap for advisers ensuring alignment with business objectives and regulatory requirements.
Conduct market research and user testing to inform product decisions and ensure a seamless user experience.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams including Product, Distribution, IT, cyber security, and UX/UI designers to define and prioritise project requirements.
Ensure timely delivery of key milestones by working alongside IT delivery and project managers.
Ensure you deliver a seamless user experience that helps drive efficiency for advisers and support staff
Communicate and present regular updates on project progress, risks, and issues to stakeholders including executive leadership team.
Ensure the project adheres to best practices in security and compliance.


Your Capability and Experience

Deep understanding of financial adviser workflows is essential
Understanding of the financial advice industry including emerging business and technology trends
Demonstrated experience as a Product Owner or similar role within the wealth/superannuation industry
Proactive problem-solving abilities, using data and insights to develop strategies, make decisions, and influence senior stakeholders
Demonstrated experience in driving & leading digital initiatives
Exceptional communication and stakeholder management skills
Proven track record of delivering outcomes in an Agile environment
Background in delivering adviser or related digital experiences


What to Expect

At CFS, you'll be working among the very best in the wealth management industry. It's an inspiring environment that encourages development and celebrates success. Other things to look forward to:

Access to CFS Employer Super, which offers an expansive investment menu and flexible insurance solutions
Support with financial services, including discounts on home loans and daily bank accounts through our partnership with a leading financial institution
Access to 24/7 confidential support covering safety, medical and mental health services
Additional leave day to celebrate your birthday
Life Leave - 3 days per annum to focus on your wellbeing or celebrate a special event
Exclusive discounts on retail, travel and entertainment


CFS Culture


At CFS we are committed to creating a thriving environment where individuals can flourish. We believe that success is built upon strong teams, and we are dedicated to celebrating uniqueness, championing individuality and supporting a diverse and inclusive workforce. We believe that when you can truly be yourself, you can unlock your full potential.

Apply today and join us in helping Australians to achieve their financial freedom.

Please note, CFS requires all candidates to have full work rights in Australia.

Where we have preferred candidates, background checks (including Police, Employment, Bankruptcy checks, ASIC banned and disqualified persons) will be completed prior to the final preferred candidate's employment being confirmed. The outcomes of the background checks do not preclude the preferred candidate, however, they will be assessed against the inherent requirements of the role.

This role is based on Gadigal Land (Sydney).

We are partnering with a national agribusiness headquartered in Brisbane seeking a Finance Business Partner - Analyst to support group-level performance, revenue analysis and strategic financial planning.

This is a commercially focused role that combines FP&A, management accounting and true business partnering. You will work closely with commercial and operational leaders to provide insight that drives revenue growth, margin performance and long-term sustainability.




The Opportunity

Reporting to the Group Manager FP&A, you will act as a key finance partner to head office stakeholders while supporting group planning, forecasting and performance analysis. The role offers exposure across revenue, pricing, cost control and capital decisions within a fast paced, operationally connected environment.

You will balance structured planning and reporting with forward looking analysis and commercial insight.

Key Responsibilities

Support annual budgeting, monthly forecasting and long range planning
Analyse revenue, pricing, sales mix and gross margin performance
Manage month end activities including accruals, recharges and reconciliations
Provide variance analysis across corporate spend and key cost drivers
Develop financial models and scenario analysis for strategic initiatives
Partner with sales and commercial leaders to support decision making
Support capital investment decisions through business case development
Drive reporting improvements, automation and process enhancement




About You

You are a commercially minded finance professional who enjoys partnering with stakeholders and translating financial data into clear insight. You are comfortable operating across both reporting and forward-looking analysis.

You will bring:

Experience in FP&A, commercial finance or financial analysis
Strong modelling capability and advanced Excel skills
Revenue and margin analysis exposure
Ability to influence and engage non finance stakeholders
CA or CPA qualified or working towards
Proactive mindset with a focus on continuous improvement




Why This Role?

Broad exposure across revenue, operations and corporate performance
True business partnering with commercial stakeholders
Opportunity to influence planning and investment decisions
National organisation with long term growth focus backed by PE
Collaborative and supportive team environment




You must hold full, unrestricted working rights in Australia.

The Opportunity

We’re partnering with a well-established and leading FMCG group with a strong presence globally. Due to internal movements, they are seeking an experienced Financial Analyst to join the team on 6-month contract. This role suits candidate on a working holiday visa.

Key Responsibilities
Prepare, analyse, and present monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reports to support decision-making
Monitor key performance indicators and highlight trends, variances, and business insights
Develop and maintain financial models to evaluate performance and guide strategy
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to gather data and deliver actionable financial insights
Support budgeting, forecasting, and pre-budget processes with accurate reporting and analysis
Conduct ad-hoc financial analysis and special projects to improve business performance
About You
2–3 years experience in a similar role
Hands-on experience in inventory-focused industries, including FMCG, manufacturing, or retail, with exposure to stock management and operational reporting
CPA/CA qualified or working towards (beneficial but not essential)
Immediately available to start
If you're interested in taking the next step in your career, please apply now or contact Jared Ooi on 02 9775 5932 or email jared.ooi@perigongroup.com.au for more information.
