Ad Text:
Systems Analyst II

Contract to 05/04/2027 | Belmont WA | Hybrid working

We are partnering with a leading Mining company to recruit an experienced Systems Analyst II. In this role you will oversee the delivery, management, enhancement, and support of tenure management systems.

Key Responsibilities

System administration, manage system use, integrity, quality and data content using tools such as Key Performance Indicators, System metrics and reporting where possible driving to self-service.

Provide hands on technical support, troubleshooting, development, improvement for tenure and contract management solutions, including improved performance and integration with other systems e.g. GIS / BI

Technical implementation, development and tracking of governance, standards etc.

Manage, improve and implement tenure and commercial workflows based on jurisdictional /business processes

Ensure action alerts are managed, received, acted on and closed. Hold users accountable

Develop and provide reporting to quality check data, completeness and report on tenure, contracts, spend, projected spend commitments etc.

Ensure all content secured according to legal requirements

Provide analytics on content to support decision making, TARPS etc. (e.g. tenure over time, tenure coming free)

What we’re looking for?

Proven experience managing and improving risk information systems and tenure/commercial management software.

Strong ability to provide quality temporal and spatial data analysis and translate insights into actionable reporting.

Experience setting and monitoring clear data governance frameworks and proactively holding users to business compliance standards.

Ability to update systems for legislative changes and integrate new jurisdictions or agreement types seamlessly.

Demonstrated success in providing ongoing support and structured training to a diverse user base.

Why apply through Lime Recruitment?

Enjoy two pay runs per week without contractor fees. As a Queensland-owned agency, we offer personalised service and are a Tier 1 supplier to the Queensland Government. Lead portfolio assurance and reporting across a large ICT investment portfolio
Influence delivery outcomes through data-driven insight and governance expertise
Flexible work options within a values-driven, inclusive organisation

 

Provide trusted portfolio insight and delivery assurance as the DMO Lead, supporting informed ICT decision-making at enterprise scale.

 

About the role

This is a full-time opportunity located in Brisbane.

The Delivery Management Office Lead supports the successful execution of Stanwell’s ICT portfolio by providing portfolio analysis, delivery assurance and integrated reporting. The role combines strong hands-on analytical capability with governance, assurance and advisory responsibility, enabling informed decision-making through accurate and timely portfolio insights across financials, schedules, risks, issues, dependencies, resourcing and benefits.

Operating with a high degree of independence, the role acts as a trusted advisor and early-warning mechanism for ICT leadership, while supporting Delivery Managers and Project Managers to uplift delivery discipline, governance compliance and portfolio maturity.

Key activities include:

Providing portfolio-level delivery assurance, performance reporting and early-warning escalation
Maintaining and analysing integrated schedules, financials, risks, issues and dependencies
Supporting portfolio budgeting, forecasting and financial closure activities
Performing quality reviews of project artefacts and governance compliance
Supporting continuous improvement of ICT delivery governance and Delivery Management maturity
Providing advisory support to Delivery Managers and Project Managers

 

 

About you

Significant experience in ICT Delivery Management Office, portfolio or delivery management roles
Strong understanding of project, program and portfolio management disciplines
Experience managing and analysing complex portfolio financials, schedules and performance data
Well-developed analytical, problem-solving and critical-thinking capabilities
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to influence and advise
Relevant qualifications in IT, project or portfolio management, engineering or equivalent experience

 

 

Benefits

Recognised as an Endorsed Employer for all Women by WORK180
Weekly pay + 12.75% super + annual incentive
Full income protection cover
Salary packaging options
Flexible work arrangements (including up to 2 days WFH)
Access to learning and development (including LinkedIn learning)
Purchased and volunteer leave options
Health and wellbeing initiatives (e.g. EAP, Fitness Passport, skin checks, flu vaccines)
Access to Bright Spark – our rewards, recognition and discounts platform
Exceptional safety culture
Plus, a whole lot more!

 

About Stanwell

Stanwell is Queensland’s largest and most reliable electricity generator. We’re applying our forty years of experience in operational excellence to building a diverse portfolio including coal-fired power stations, renewable generation, storage, and firming to deliver Queensland’s future energy needs. Experience an organisation that lives by its values to care, adapt and deliver and is constantly responding to create an environment where respect, inclusivity, safety, and wellbeing are delivered.

 

What’s next?

Applications close: 11:55pm, 09 April 2026

Further information on Stanwell can be found online at www.stanwell.com

As part of the recruitment process, you may be required to undertake pre-employment checks (may include police checks, AusCheck, medical assessment and/or drug and alcohol testing). 

Inclusion and diversity is an important part of shaping Stanwell’s culture and how our workplace operates. We recognise and value that it’s essential to creating a safe, inclusive, and productive workplace.

We are proactive about giving our people opportunities to excel and truly be themselves. We enable this through diversity, fairness, and flexible work practices.

Through our dedicated colleagues, CVTS delivers world class ticketing services and solutions – creating exceptional outcomes for our client and the community who count on them. You have an opportunity to personally thrive, make a difference and be part of a culture where individuality is noticed and valued every day.

About the Role

We are seeking a detail-oriented Product Analyst to join our team on a 6-month contract role. You’ll turn data into insights that drive strategic and operational decision‑making. You’ll collaborate with cross‑functional teams to support continuous improvement and help optimise business performance.

Key Responsibilities

Collect, clean, and analyse large datasets from multiple sources.

Develop and maintain dashboards, reports, and visualisations to cleary communicate insights.

Identify trends, patterns, and anomalies in data to support strategic initiatives and operational improvements.

Collaborate with stakeholders to understand business needs and translate them into data solutions.

Monitor data quality and ensure data integrity.

Contribute to data collection processes and strategies that enhance efficiency and statistical reliability.

Job Title
Business Analyst Job Description


Rabobank is the world’s leading specialist in food & agribusiness banking.  One of our key strengths lies in our people who have a deep understanding of agriculture & are committed to adding long-term value for clients.  Our commitment to our employees & clients is at the heart of everything we do. 

About the role


Rabobank is looking for a Business Analyst to join our team in Sydney. The Business Analyst performs business analysis functions in initiatives to support outcomes for the bank. In this role you will collaborate closely with Squads and teams and other stakeholders from within the business,  third parties, as well as other initiatives where necessary.

This role sits within the COO domain and is made up of Technology, Operations, Chief Product Owner, Security & Monitoring, Financial & Economic Crime Unit and Shared Services. Working within the COO Domain will mean working together with other Teams to provide the best overall services to both internal and external stakeholders.

Key Responsibilities and Accountabilities 


Working with the Senior Business Analyst and/or Product Owners to define and deliver business initiatives

Lead business analysis activities, including discovery, requirements gathering and solution design

Facilitate workshops and working groups to capture business needs and cross-functional requirements

Develop current and future state process models and assess impacts and opportunities

Translate business needs into clear business requirements, user stories and functional specifications

Support solution delivery through testing, risk identification and implementation activities

Contribute to change, training and adoption activities to ensure successful outcomes

To be successful in the role, you will have:


Experience within a financial services, risk or compliance environment

Experience working across Agile, hybrid or waterfall delivery models, with a strong preference for Agile ways of working

Strong analytical skills, with the ability to simplify complex concepts

Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to present complex concepts to both technical and non‑technical stakeholders

Good time management and collaboration

What we're proud to offer you:


Rabo@Anywhere is our hybrid way of working at Rabobank and for Australia, that means we have a blend of time working between the office and home. 

Wellbeing leave. These leave days support greater work/life balance and is just another way we are helping our employees to lead happier, healthier, and more fulfilling lives.

Education Assistance Program. Rabobank values the development of its people and has a great Education Assistance Program to assist with professional development.

Parental Leave that supports you and your family while giving you the freedom and flexibility to enjoy this special time (up-to 14 weeks paid leave)

Bonus and Additional Leave. We realise sometimes 4 weeks Annual Leave isn't enough! At Rabobank we provide an incentive for eligible employees to receive 1 extra week Annual Leave and an option to purchase another 2 weeks Annual Leave.

Extra Insurance. Rabobank recognises that employees need to protect their financial wellbeing, in the event of serious illness, injury, or even death. Rabobank provides eligible permanent employees with employer-funded Death, TPD and Income Protection Insurance.

Rabo Workplace Giving program. As part of Rabobank's global Corporate Social Responsibility, Workplace Giving aims to encourage employees to contribute to our community. Rabo Workplace Giving program matches employee donations to selected Social Partners.

Our Values


Rabobank Australia values inclusion, belonging, and positive experiences for all. Our work environment, our benefits, and the way we live our values, “Client Driven”, “Responsible”, “Professional” and “Cooperative” make it a great place to work. We welcome applicants from diverse backgrounds.

Please let our Talent Acquisition team know if you need any accommodations to make our opportunities more accessible to you.

Rabobank is not accepting submissions from recruitment agencies for this role.

Applicants must hold valid work rights for this role

Job Title
Senior Business Analyst Job Description


Rabobank is the world’s leading specialist in food & agribusiness banking.  One of our key strengths lies in our people who have a deep understanding of agriculture & are committed to adding long-term value for clients. Our commitment to our employees & clients is at the heart of everything we do. 

About the role


Rabobank is seeking an experienced Senior Business Analyst to join our team in Sydney. The Senior Business Analyst leads business analysis functions in bank initiatives to support the implementation of business outcomes. They work as part of a Squad or team. They may guide, mentor and lead junior business analysts, testers and other team members in the execution of their functions that concentrate around business analysis deliverables.

This role sits within the COO domain and is made up of Technology, Operations, Chief Product Owner, Security & Monitoring, Financial & Economic Crime Unit and Shared Services. Working within the COO Domain will mean working together with other Teams to provide the best overall services to both internal and external stakeholders.

Key Responsibilities and Accountabilities 


Develop and manage the business analysis approach, plan and deliverables, reporting progress, risks and dependencies to the Product Owner and senior stakeholders.

Identify and engage key stakeholders, facilitate workshops and senior working groups, and build strong relationships to validate outcomes and provide strategic, enterprise‑level input.

Contribute to defining initiative scope, objectives and outcomes, undertaking discovery activities including regulatory analysis, gap analysis, synergies and market opportunity assessment.

Produce unambiguous business requirements, user stories and functional specifications to support policy, process, organisational, digital and data change initiatives.

Contribute to the development of target operating models (TOMs) and solution options in collaboration with architecture, Product Owners and Product Leads, providing clear recommendations.

Support implementation through quality assurance of solutions, UAT/BAT, process and policy updates, training materials, and broader change activities.

Maintain end‑to‑end traceability, assurance and reporting (including board and committee materials), ensuring all documentation meets organisational and banking framework standards.

To be successful in the role, you will have:


Extensive experience as a Senior Business Analyst within a financial services, risk or compliance environment

Strong experience working on complex, multi‑workstream initiatives within an agile delivery environment

A strong understanding of digital enablement and automation solutions, including process modelling and optimisation

Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to tailor messaging and present complex concepts to both technical and non‑technical stakeholders

What we're proud to offer you:


Rabo@Anywhere is our hybrid way of working at Rabobank and for Australia, that means we have a blend of time working between the office and home. 

Wellbeing leave. These leave days support greater work/life balance and is just another way we are helping our employees to lead happier, healthier, and more fulfilling lives.

Education Assistance Program. Rabobank values the development of its people and has a great Education Assistance Program to assist with professional development.

Parental Leave that supports you and your family while giving you the freedom and flexibility to enjoy this special time (up-to 14 weeks paid leave)

Ad Text:
Mirvac is a brand that creates and curates better experiences. We are more than developers or builders - we are visionaries, and our ability to see the world differently drives us to be bold, embrace innovation and diversity, and lead with optimism.

As creators of positive change, we see each new project as an opportunity to leave a lasting legacy, delivering enduring value for all Australians. We focus on sustainability, innovation, and shared value, to return benefits to our customers, partners, investors, and communities.

The Director, Valuations provides enterprise-wide leadership, strategic direction and oversight for the valuation function, ensuring the integrity, consistency and appropriate application of valuations across the commercial, retail, industrial and build-to-rent asset classes.

The role sits within the Asset Management team and reports to the General Manager, Strategy and Operations. The Director, Valuations supports owners whilst working closely with the Funds Management and Finance teams.The Director Valuations ensures valuation outcomes support capital allocation, portfolio strategy and long-term value creation, while meeting governance, regulatory and fiduciary obligations. Utilise valuation intelligence to actively support forward looking portfolio insights, alongside the asset management team, that drive long-term risk-adjusted returns.

This role sets the vision, frameworks, operating model and culture for the valuation function, positioning it as a trusted adviser to senior leaders, committees and boards, ensuring valuation insights are leveraged to drive informed, forward-looking decision making across the portfolio.

Your responsibilities will include:

Enterprise Leadership & Culture


Provide visible, values-led leadership to the valuations function, fostering a culture of accountability, independence, commercial acumen and continuous improvement.
Build, develop and mentor senior valuation leaders and specialists, ensuring strong capability, succession depth and alignment to future portfolio needs.
Champion ethical decision-making, professional judgement and valuation independence across all funds and asset classes.

Strategic Valuation Leadership


Set and own the enterprise valuation strategy and frameworks, and standards, ensuring consistency of approach while recognising asset-specific and sector-specific drivers.
Provide strategic insight on valuation trends, market movements and emerging risks, translating technical outcomes into clear executive-level implications.
Act as a trusted adviser on valuation matters across acquisitions, divestments, capital allocation, development feasibility and portfolio strategy

Executive & Stakeholder Engagement


Serve as the primary senior interface with Executive Leadership, Investment Committees, Valuation Committees, Boards, auditors, regulators and key external valuation firms.
Lead complex and sensitive stakeholder discussions on valuation outcomes, judgement calls and market movements, particularly where outcomes are contested or require escalation.
Build and maintain strong, strategic relationships with external valuation partners to ensure quality, independence, consistency and innovation.

Governance, Risk & Compliance


Maintain ultimate accountability for compliance with fund valuation policies, regulatory standards and governance frameworks.
Oversee and lead the preparation, sign-off and presentation of Valuation Committee and Board papers, ensuring valuation movements, drivers and implications are clearly articulated.
Sponsor periodic reviews of valuation policies, methodologies and controls to ensure they remain fit for purpose in evolving market and regulatory environments.
Maintain oversight of valuation model risk, key assumptions, sensitivities, and scenario analysis, ensuring transparency, documentation, and auditability.

Portfolio Insight & Value Creation


Oversee valuation outcomes across the full portfolio, ensuring consistency while enabling informed decision-making at asset, fund and enterprise level.
Partner with Asset and Fund teams to identify value-enhancing initiatives, capital optimisation opportunities and risk mitigations informed by valuation insights.
Ensure valuation data and analysis are effectively leveraged to support performance assessment, strategy formulation and capital decisions.

Operating Model & Continuous Improvement


Design and oversee the valuation operating model, balancing rigour, efficiency and responsiveness to business needs.
Drive continuous improvement in valuation processes, data quality, systems and analytics to enhance insight, scalability and decision-making.
Explore and promote innovation and technology in valuation approaches, modelling techniques and market intelligence, including being across emerging technology, its potential application and value
Be well informed on emerging technologies and how they can be ultilised to streamline processes.

Experience


15+ years' senior experience across property valuation, funds management, investment or related roles, with deep exposure to complex, institutional portfolios.
Proven experience leading senior professionals and enterprise-level functions.
Experience working with landlords and owners to deliver valuations outcomes
Demonstrated ability to influence Executive, Committee and Board-level stakeholders.

Qualifications


Tertiary qualifications in property, finance, economics or a related discipline.
Professional valuation credentials required.
Finance or investment credentials highly regarded.

Leadership Attributes


Strategic, commercially astute leader with the ability to balance independence with partnership.
Highly credible communicator able to translate complex valuation concepts into clear, actionable business insights.
Confident decision-maker who operates effectively in ambiguous and high-stakes environments.
Values-driven leader who demonstrates integrity, curiosity, accountability and collaboration.

All of these attributes are preferred, if you think you may be the right fit for the role please still apply.

A leading federal government agency is seeking an AI Business Analyst to help deliver and govern responsible AI‑enabled features and services. In this role, you will work closely with multidisciplinary agile teams to translate business problems into clear, testable user stories and acceptance criteria, ensuring AI features are transparent, ethical, compliant and supported by strong human‑in‑the‑loop controls.

This position offers the opportunity to shape AI‑enabled processes, uplift organisational capability and contribute to safe, effective and scalable use of AI technologies.

Key Responsibilities

Elicit, analyse and document business, data and AI requirements; decompose into epics, features and user stories with testable acceptance criteria.
Define AI‑specific, non‑functional requirements (transparency, safety, robustness, latency, privacy, security).
Map business processes and decision points to determine AI vs rules‑based logic, including human authority levels and HITL checkpoints.
Co‑design evaluation frameworks for AI features, including datasets, baselines, performance monitoring, drift, fairness and quality.
Collaborate with data scientists, engineers and product owners to translate desired outcomes into model objectives, features and data constraints.
Lead workshops, discovery sessions and backlog refinement; produce service blueprints, journey maps and future‑state workflows.
Develop acceptance criteria and test cases for AI/ML features (shadow testing, A/B trials, red teaming, rollback plans).
Document model/prompt assumptions, guardrails, redaction rules, retrieval scope and safety constraints.
Contribute to privacy and risk assessments, including PIAs, threat assessments and governance documentation for AI artefacts.
Ensure alignment with internal AI policy, AI governance frameworks and data governance standards.
Support change management, communications and training for AI‑enabled processes and tools.
Participate in communities of practice and continuous improvement activities.

Qualifications & Experience

3+ years' experience as a Business Analyst or Systems Analyst in digital service delivery and agile environments.
Practical experience delivering or supporting AI‑enabled products within the Microsoft ecosystem (ML, NLP/LLMs, Copilot, intelligent automation).
Ability to write clear, testable user stories and acceptance criteria for AI features, including evaluation metrics and guardrails.
Knowledge of responsible and secure AI principles (privacy‑by‑design, human oversight, explainability, bias mitigation).
Strong workshop facilitation and stakeholder engagement skills with both technical and non‑technical stakeholders.
Proficiency with Azure DevOps, Miro or equivalent tools.
Excellent written and verbal communication, with an ability to influence decision‑making.

Essential Criteria

(Candidates must provide responses, up to 3000 characters each)

Demonstrated capability, knowledge, experience and clearances (if applicable) to meet role requirements.
Proven experience delivering similar AI, data or digital analysis services.
Demonstrated capacity to deliver and manage services over the proposed term.
Ability to deliver positive social or environmental impact.
Competitive pricing and value for money.
A risk profile aligned with organisational requirements.

Job Title: Head of Digital Product & Design, IT
Ad Text:
Head of Digital Product and Design, IT
Sydney (Broadway) or Fremantle (WA) Campus
Fixed Term contract (12 months – parental leave to April 2027), Full-time

Friendly and collaborative environment

Rewarding opportunity

Level 10 + 14.5% superannuation

 

About the university

We believe in the power of education to transform lives, strengthen communities and help people become the best version of themselves. The University of Notre Dame Australia is a modern, national Catholic university that serves more than 12,000 students across its campuses in Fremantle, Broome and Sydney.

With a community of over 1,000 academic and professional staff, Notre Dame is recognised for its personalised learning environment, strong graduate outcomes and holistic educational philosophy rooted in critical thinking, ethics and the Catholic intellectual tradition.

About the office

The Information Technology Office plays an integral role in the success of the University, ensuring the national provision of technology solutions that enable Learning & Teaching, Research, and Administration across our University.
 
About the role

The Head of Digital Product and Design leads the university’s digital transformation by shaping strategy for web and portal platforms and delivering innovative, user‑centred experiences. The role drives engagement through strong UX/CX design, user research, data‑driven insights, and collaborative cross‑functional leadership.

Key Responsibilities

Execute the digital experience strategy in alignment with university priorities.

Serve as the primary advisor on UX/UI best practices, defining KPIs and reporting on UX impact.

Develop and implement research and data strategies to guide UX decisions, using analytics and user insights to drive continuous improvement.

Lead and mentor a multidisciplinary team to translate requirements into effective digital solutions and foster a collaborative, innovative culture.

Partner with the Digital Transformation team to embed UX within transformation initiatives and support organisational change.

Deliver user‑centred, accessible digital experiences that meet strategic, business, and technical requirements.

Manage external vendor relationships to ensure high‑quality digital solution delivery.

Oversee monitoring and proactive management of digital platform performance, availability, and reliability.

Identify opportunities for innovation through monitoring digital trends and emerging technologies.

Ensure digital compliance with accessibility, privacy, and security standards.

Establish agile, iterative processes for prototyping, testing, and validation.

Lead user research, usability testing, and data analysis to inform design and development.

Embed audience‑led design through research and analytics to enhance engagement and usability.

Manage the digital product and design budget to ensure effective and cost‑efficient operations.

Act as a key stakeholder during unforeseen technical or UX issues, leading troubleshooting efforts and risk mitigation to minimise disruption.

 

 

Qualifications, skills & experience

Bachelor’s degree in digital technologies, interaction design, UX, service design or similar.

Significant experience leading UX teams across diverse digital projects, including guiding teams through change.

Proven ability to manage multidisciplinary teams (researchers, analysts, designers, developers, product owners) in complex environments such as higher education.

Strong track record delivering data‑driven, user‑centred digital solutions.

Experience with Agile, service design methodologies, and modern digital product development.

Demonstrated application of accessibility and inclusive design principles (WCAG compliance).

Expertise in UX strategy, processes, DesignOps and/or ResearchOps.

Experience managing digital product/design budgets and resources.

Skilled in crisis management and resolving technical issues under pressure.

Working knowledge of front‑ and back‑end languages (HTML, CSS, JavaScript).

Deep understanding of human‑centred design, usability testing, and accessibility standards.

Ability to measure UX impact using KPIs and success metrics that support organisational goals.

Experience embedding UX practices within digital transformation initiatives.

Proficiency in wireframing, prototyping, UI design, journey mapping, persona development, and user research/testing.

Skilled in using digital analytics tools (e.g., Google Analytics, Hotjar) to inform design decisions.

Strong troubleshooting, contingency planning, and issue‑resolution abilities.

Familiarity with modern digital tools, platforms and analytics software.

  

How to apply

Please apply online with a resume and cover letter outlining your suitability for the role.

Applications closing date: 7 April 2026

For further information on how to apply, please visit: How to apply | Notre Dame

Applicants are expected to have current and valid work rights in Australia.

Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people are encouraged to apply.     

The University is committed to providing a safe, respectful and inclusive working and learning environment.

The successful candidate will be required to complete a self-declaration regarding Gender-Based Violence (as required by the National Code) as part of the pre-employment process. For more information on this please refer to our website.

The University of Notre Dame Australia seeks to increase the diversity of our workforce to better meet the different needs of the University and its stakeholders and to improve equal opportunity outcomes for our staff.

Please direct your queries regarding this role to Janette Tam, janette.tam@nd.edu.au

Unsolicited contact from recruitment agencies will not be entertained.

Job Title: Practice Manager - CX and Conversational AI
Salary: Practice Manager CX and Conversational AI
Ad Text:
Our Why 

Datacom works with organisations and communities across Australia and New Zealand to make a difference in people’s lives and help organisations use the power of tech to innovate and grow.

About the Role (Your Why) 

At Datacom, we connect people and technology to solve challenges, create opportunities, and unlock new possibilities for the communities we serve. Our mission is simple: make a meaningful difference through the power of innovation. We partner with organisations and communities across Australia and New Zealand to improve lives and help businesses use technology to innovate, grow, and succeed.

As a Conversational AI & CX leader you will blend hands-on technical expertise with commercial acumen and the ability to engage deeply with customers at every level. You’ll design, build, showcase and optimise cutting-edge conversational solutions that inspire and deliver measurable business value, driving improved customer and employee experiences.

If you’re passionate about customers and obsessed with designing human-centric experiences that deliver ROI, this is your stage. You see the bigger picture, yet dive into the details, and you’re recognised as a thought leader and practitioner in the CX and EX space.

About the team

You’ll be part of our dynamic, fast-growing Conversational AI team across ANZ and Asia, collaborating with some of the most innovative and recognisable brands in Australia and New Zealand. In this pivotal role, you’ll help shape next-generation service experiences by applying Conversational AI and broader AI capabilities to transform customer engagement. Your ability to navigate ambiguity and guide teams through technical and organisational complexity will be essential to driving impactful outcomes across a broad range of industries.

We value flexibility – our offices are based in Adelaide, Brisbane, Canberra, Melbourne, Perth, Sydney, – and we support working from home too. We empower you and your team to balance collaboration and work-life harmony. If you’re an innovative thinker who thrives at the intersection of technology and customer outcomes, you’ll inspire clients with visionary ideas while delivering tangible, high-quality solutions. Your collaborative approach, technical credibility, and passion for CX/EX will make you a trusted advisor and a driver of change.

What You’ll Do

We work in an agile environment where no two days are the same – responding to evolving customer, technology, and business needs. You will:

Create compelling, customer-focused experiences powered by Conversational AI technologies and be comfortable presenting and discussing these with customers and prospects
Ideate, design, build, and deliver powerful demonstrations that showcase CAI capabilities to existing and prospective clients
Apply advanced conversational design principles to craft intuitive, human-centric interaction flows
Transition seamlessly between deep technical conversations with engineers and strategic discussions with business leaders
Collaborate with customers to shape their CX and EX visions – inspiring new ideas and provoking thoughts about the ‘art of the possible’
Identify automation opportunities in contact centre and retail environments that deliver ROI while enhancing frontline operations
Manage and optimise delivery of current solutions including intent management, conversational structure and integration with customers' digital environments
Work with customers to identify tuning and refining opportunities to increase bot containment and improve agent and customer satisfaction within risk tolerance and accessibility standards
Work effectively across stakeholders to translate ambitious customer visions into practical, scalable solutions
Apply change management methodologies and agile delivery techniques to ensure seamless adoption and lasting impact
Plan and integrate next‑generation technologies into the architectural strategy and build strategic relationships with software and hardware vendors to inform roadmaps and GTM approaches

What you’ll bring

Required Experience

Proven (3+ years) expertise in Conversational AI and CX strategy delivering measurable improvements
Strong understanding of automation in contact centre and retail environments
Ability to lead and contribute to project management and implementation activities
Exceptional stakeholder engagement and communication skills, able to navigate technical and business conversations
Experience with platforms such as Twilio, Cognigy, Genesys, and Microsoft
Proven leadership experience and excellent presentation and stakeholder management skills
Experience in solution and design services, scoping, configuration assistance and needs assessments

Nice to Have

Strong working knowledge of change management and agile delivery frameworks
Background in outsourcing or working with systems integrators
Experience with Prosci Change Management methodology
Familiarity with ITIL, application management and integration into core business systems
Understanding of key cloud vendors such as AWS, Azure, GCP and commercial awareness of vendor architectural strategies

Why join us here at Datacom?

Datacom is one of Australia and New Zealand’s largest suppliers of Information Technology professional services, while maintaining a dynamic, agile, small-business feel. It's our people that give Datacom its unique culture and energy.

At Datacom you'll be recognised and valued for your contributions. We're growing year on year and can provide stability, career opportunity and a collegial, agile, flat-structured environment that empowers people and promotes autonomy. We care about our people and provide a range of perks such as social events, chill-out spaces, remote working, flexi-hours and professional development courses to name a few. We operate at the leading edge of technology to help ANZ’s largest enterprise organisations explore possibilities and solve their greatest challenges, so you will never run out of interesting new challenges and opportunities.

If you would like to know more and this sounds like you. Please apply online today!

Wolfe Systems is growing its Dynamics capability and we are looking for a practical, customer-focused Dynamics 365 Business Central Consultant to join the team.

This role is ideal for someone who understands Business Central well, enjoys working closely with customers, and can help turn business requirements into practical ERP outcomes. You’ll be involved across discovery, scoping, implementation, migration planning, training and ongoing customer guidance.

This is not a pure developer role and it is not a purely strategic consulting role. We are looking for someone who can work hands-on with customers, understand how businesses operate, and help guide successful Business Central implementations from early planning through to adoption.


The Role

You will work with customers to understand their operational processes and requirements, then support the design and delivery of Business Central solutions that align to those needs.

You’ll be involved in implementation planning, solution configuration, migration readiness, process mapping, user training and general customer guidance. You will also work with internal stakeholders and technical resources where integrations, extensions or development inputs are required.

This is a delivery-focused role with strong customer interaction. It will suit someone who is comfortable asking the right questions, guiding customers through change and helping build structured, scalable ERP outcomes.




Key Responsibilities

Work with customers to understand business processes and operational requirements

Support pre-sales discovery, scoping and implementation planning

Configure and support delivery of Dynamics 365 Business Central solutions

Assist with process alignment across finance, purchasing, sales, inventory and warehousing

Support data migration readiness, planning and validation

Train end users and customer stakeholders in Business Central and related processes

Produce clear implementation, process and handover documentation

Work with internal developers or technical resources where required

Support customer adoption and continuous improvement after go-live

Travel to customer sites where required for workshops, implementation and training




What We’re Looking For

3+ years of relevant experience with Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central or closely related ERP environments

Strong functional understanding of Business Central, particularly across finance, purchasing, sales and warehousing

Experience working with customers in implementation, consulting or solution delivery environments

Ability to understand customer operations and translate them into practical system outcomes

Experience supporting data migration planning and implementation readiness

Strong communication skills and confidence in customer-facing discussions

Ability to deliver user training and support customer adoption

Strong organisational skills and ability to manage multiple delivery activities




Desirables

Experience with manufacturing workflows or manufacturing-related ERP processes

Experience with integrations, extensions or leading development inputs

Familiarity with Power Platform, automation or related Microsoft tooling

Microsoft Dynamics / Business Central certifications

Experience in mid-market customer environments

Experience working in a consulting, partner or managed services environment




What We Offer

Opportunity to help shape and grow Wolfe Systems’ Dynamics capability

Strong customer-facing role with real influence on delivery outcomes

Exposure to a range of customer environments and business processes

Supportive team environment with room to grow

Competitive salary based on experience

Opportunity to continue building Dynamics and broader Microsoft business application capability

Job Title: Domain Solution Architect – Mining, Oil & Gas
Ad Text:
DXC Technology (NYSE: DXC) is a leading enterprise technology and innovation partner delivering software, services, and solutions to global enterprises and public sector organizations — helping them harness AI to drive outcomes at a time of exponential change with speed. With deep expertise in Managed Infrastructure Services, Application Modernization, and Industry-Specific Software Solutions, DXC modernizes, secures, and operates some of the world’s most complex technology estates. Learn more on dxc.com

The Domain Solution Architect – Mining, Oil & Gas is a senior leadership role accountable for shaping, governing, and delivering analytics, AI enabled and digital transformation solutions primarily across Mining, Oil & Gas clients.

The role combines industry interlock leadership, solution architecture authority, presales accountability, and commercial ownership to drive sustainable revenue growth and ensure DXC delivers high‑quality, scalable, and operationally practical solutions within asset‑intensive and safety‑critical environments.

Key Responsibilities

Industry & Client Leadership

Act as Data & AI’s senior domain authority for Mining, Oil & Gas across Western Australia.

Build and maintain trusted advisor relationships with C‑suite, executive, and senior operational stakeholders including DXC’s internal client partners and sellers.

Provide industry insight and thought leadership to influence client strategy, investment decisions, and technology roadmaps.

Represent DXC in executive engagements, industry forums, and strategic client discussions.

Revenue Growth & Portfolio Ownership

Own and grow the Mining, Oil & Gas Data & AI’s portfolio, including pipeline, revenue, and total contract value.

Partner with Industry, Sales, and Account leaders to identify, qualify, and prioritise opportunities.

Lead strategic pursuits, account expansions, and large‑scale bids.

Improve win rates through disciplined opportunity qualification, value‑led solution framing, and strong solution governance.

Presales, Solution Architecture & Governance

The Opportunity

Reporting to the Head of Business Improvement, the Process Excellence Lead helps ElectraNet improve how work gets done by designing simpler, more efficient and customer‑focused end‑to‑end processes.

Working closely with leaders and cross‑functional teams, the role applies lean thinking, service design and data‑driven analysis to understand current‑state processes, identify inefficiencies and design practical, sustainable improvements.

This is a hands‑on process design role. The Process Excellence Lead focuses on doing the deep process work — translating business needs into tangible artefacts and supporting teams to implement improved ways of working.

Key aspects of the role include:

Mapping complex end‑to‑end processes using customer journey mapping, service blueprinting and value stream mapping
Designing future‑state processes that improve flow, reduce waste and enhance customer and employee experience
Analysing cycle times, hand‑offs, pain points and inefficiencies using qualitative and quantitative data
Translating insights into clear artefacts such as journey maps, service blueprints, process documentation and requirements
Facilitating workshops and working sessions to align stakeholders and co‑design solutions
Measuring success through improved flow, simplified processes and measurable operational outcomes
About You

This role suits a process specialist who enjoys getting into the detail and improving how work actually gets done.

You may come from a background such as business analysis, process excellence, lean operations, service design or operational transformation, and have demonstrated experience designing and improving complex processes across multiple stakeholder groups.

We're looking for someone who:

Has strong experience in customer journey mapping and /or service blueprinting, including personas and multiple perspectives (e.g. customer, employee, technology, leaders)
Is comfortable working with complex, cross‑functional processes and identifying waste, hand‑offs and bottlenecks
Applies lean principles pragmatically to improve flow and efficiency
Is curious, patient and resilient, and consistently challenges legacy ways of working to rethink processes
Can facilitate sessions and influence stakeholders to think differently about processes
Enjoys translating ambiguity into clear, usable artefacts
